hero_subtitle: SFTY structures and routes multi-provider sanctions and adverse media screening outputs into auditable decisions with case management and evidence preservation for compliance teams.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Compliance decisions stall with disconnected screening outputs
problem_description: Compliance teams face delays and inconsistent risk assessment when sanctions and adverse media screening results remain siloed across multiple providers. This fragmentation makes it difficult to create clear, auditable records that support defensible compliance decisions and streamlined review workflows.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY links sanctions and adverse media results from multiple providers into one workflow that applies rules, creates cases, records reviewer actions and preserves audit-ready evidence for compliance teams.
workflow_title: From screening alerts to compliance decisions
workflow_description: SFTY routes sanctions and adverse media screening outputs through rules and reviews to create documented cases, record decisions and preserve audit-ready evidence.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented screening. One decision layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY turns sanctions and adverse media outputs into structured reviews, documented decisions, routed cases and audit-ready evidence without replacing existing screening providers.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY enables VASP teams to centralize sanctions screening outputs into structured, audit-ready decisions with controlled access and enterprise-grade deployment readiness.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for sanctions screening teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a compliance control layer that connects sanctions screening outputs into structured reviews, cases, decisions and reports for VASP screening and compliance teams.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle adverse media screening workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY turns adverse media alerts into structured cases, applies internal rules, routes reviews, tracks decisions and supports evidence collection within a connected workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready sanctions evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves decision logs, case history and evidence packages to provide a clear audit trail of sanctions screening reviews and compliance actions.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate multiple screening providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY orchestrates outputs from multiple sanctions and adverse media providers, normalizing results and creating one operational compliance workflow.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions screening providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY connects existing sanctions and screening providers and structures their outputs without replacing them.
final_title: Turn screening outputs into audit-ready cases
final_description: See how SFTY structures sanctions and adverse media results into controlled workflows, evidence packages and compliance decisions.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
